curator_log.notes: install_config already has 6 behavioral F2P tests targeting the StepMeta→StepSpec merge (test_runner_executes_pipeline, test_runner_respects_dependency_order, test_runner_saves_artifact_automatically, test_resolve_executor_step_preserves_deps, test_artifact_save_and_load_typed, test_artifact_roundtrip_through_pipeline). These all reference the flattened API (step.deps, step.output_path) and would fail at base where StepSpec uses nested meta=StepMeta(...). Caveat: the instruction asks to delete tests/test_step.py and lib/marin/src/marin/execution/step.py but the canonical_patch (files_changed_count=6, no D entries, _n_mutating_ops=30) does not capture file deletions — instruction-vs-canonical-patch divergence on deletions is a known limitation of tool_replay reconstruction, not blocking since the F2P verifier doesn't depend on those deletions.
